session_label: Teece 2016 candidate-corpus reachability probe — Digital Bodleian + YEK
scope: 7 candidate manuscripts identified from Teece 2016 thesis as Pir Budaq Qaraqoyunlu library members; reachability + cataloguer-metadata capture; substantive Hakîkî-content textual search deferred to follow-on session.

method_summary.primary_bodleian: direct HTTP against digital.bodleian.ox.ac.uk public search; application/ld+json Accept header used for the public Data API at /search/
method_summary.fallback_bodleian: Playwright headless browser navigation, 60s navigation cap
method_summary.secondary_bodleian_catalogue: Fihrist public catalogue records checked for Ouseley 131, 140, 141
method_summary.primary_yek: scripts/yek_search.mjs CLI with .env auth (from Decorated Manuscripts project); dedup_registry.json was checked read-only and had no exact prior runs for the requested query strings

probes.source_reference: Teece 2016, pp. 52 (and Chapter 2 detailed analysis); preliminary main-session read at PDF p. 93

probe_attempts.outcome: HTML application shell; no challenge markers
application/ld+json search returned totalItems=0
Rendered search page reported: No items found
Catalogue record found

probes.notes: Teece target was described here as Süleymaniye with no shelfmark, but the live YEK precision match is TIEM 02030, not a Süleymaniye collection row. Targeted Süleymaniye field checks rendered zero rows/null counts. No viewer link/IIIF availability surfaced.

probes.source_reference: Teece 2016, Appendix B entry #26 (printed pp. ~294 to 296): "Fatih 4054 | Kulliyat of Imad al-Din (d. 1371) | date TBD | Istanbul, Suleymaniye Mosque Library"
probes.probe_purpose: Follow-up verification of Teece Appendix B entry #26 against B4 finding (TIEM 02030 = Pir-Budaq-era Imad-i Faqih Kulliyat). Resolves Codex Call 2 note: "B4 live precision hit resolves to TIEM 02030, not a confirmed Süleymaniye record."

probe_attempts.outcome: Süleymaniye Fatih 4054 resolves to a 7-component Imad-i Faqih Kulliyat composite
Detail metadata extracted for all 7 components; uniform date_ah 898, 21 lines per page, 340x172 mm, Talik script

best_metadata_capture.title: Külliyat of Imad al-Din Faqih Kermani (multi-text composite)
best_metadata_capture.contents_summary: Component breakdown: 04054-001 Fâtihatü'l-İhlâs (ff.1-36, YEK 242536); 04054-002 Sohbetnâme (ff.37-63, YEK 242537); 04054-003 Muhabbetnâme (ff.64-89, YEK 242538); 04054-004 Misbâhu'l-Hidâye (ff.90-163, YEK 242539); 04054-005 Dehnâme (ff.164-183, YEK 242540); 04054-006 Dîvân-ı Kasâid (ff.184-208, YEK 242541); 04054-007 Dîvân-ı Gazeliyyât (ff.209-400, YEK 242542).

probes.notes: Teece Appendix B entry #26 lists Fatih 4054 with date TBD. YEK detail-page colophon resolves to 898 AH / 1492-93 CE, 26 years after Pir Budaq's death in 871/1466. So Teece's Pir-Budaq-era attribution applies to TIEM 02030 (the B4 precision hit, dated 863/1459), not to Fatih 4054. Both manuscripts exist as independent witnesses of the same Imad-i Faqih text tradition but in different patronage contexts. See notes/teece_2016_appendix_b_disambiguation.md for the full disambiguation narrative.

major_quirks: Digital Bodleian default HTML is an application shell; application/ld+json Accept header is needed for direct JSON search results.
YEK AllFields tokenizes multi-word shelfmark strings and produced very large noisy counts; precision searches used yer_numarasi plus collection/title/author anchors.
YEK detail extraction did not expose structured scribe/calligrapher fields for B2/B3, even where Teece supplies copyist attributions.

B4 live precision hit (TIEM 02030) and B5 follow-up verification (Süleymaniye Fatih 04054) confirm two independent manuscripts of the Imad-i Faqih Kulliyat text tradition: TIEM 02030 dated 04 Zilhicce 863 AH / 1459 CE (Pir-Budaq-era) and Fatih 04054 dated 898 AH / 1492-93 CE (post-Pir-Budaq). Teece Appendix B entry #26 date_TBD placeholder resolves to the later copy via YEK colophon.
